OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY PLTF IN OPPOSITION TO RANDALL B. WEIL, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT WEIL, RANDALL B.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RANDALL B.WEIL, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF HAD THE RIGHT TO RELY ON DR. WEIL'S ASSURANCES (SEE UNJIAN V. BERMAN (1989) 208 CAL. APP. 3D 881) AND HER DECLARATION IS NOT SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO STRIKE UNDER D'AMICO. AS A CONSEQUENCE THE CLAIM IS NOT BARRED BY CCP 340.5. DR. HVIESTENDAHL'S DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE DECLARATION STATES WHAT THE WITNESS RELIED UPON AND SINCE HE PERFORMED SURGERY ON PLAINTIFF, DR. HVIESTENDAHL WAS IN POSITION TO UNDERSTAND THE PROBLEMS WITH THE PRIOR SURGERY. ALTHOUGH THE OPINION SET FORTH IS NOT EXPRESSED IN STANDARD PHRASES, THE IMPORT IS SUFFICIENT TO DEFEAT A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WHERE ALL INFERENCES ARE TO BE GIVEN TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE. ORDER ON MOTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS, ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: OFF CALENDAR, CASE SETTLED PER PLTF'S LETTER DATED 11-13-08. (D220)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RANDALL B.WEIL, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: David J. Pullman, Esq. of Law Office of David Pullman 866/244-5004 for pltf & opposing party; Joseph C. Gharrity, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP 288-9800 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff presents evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff had the right to rely on Dr. Weil's assurances (see Unjian v. Berman (1989) 208 Cal. App. 3d 881) and her declaration is not subject to a motion to strike under D'Amico. As a consequence the claim is not barred by CCP 340.5. Dr. Hviestendahl's declaration is admissible to create a triable issue of fact. The declaration states what the witness relied upon and since he performed surgery on plaintiff, Dr. Hviestendahl was in position to understand the problems with the prior surgery. Although the opinion set forth is not expressed in standard phrases, the import is sufficient to defeat a motion for summary judgment where all inferences are to be given to plaintiff's evidence. Order on motion signed in open court. Court ruled on defendant's evidentiary objections, order signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
